subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: Explain the types of soil in India
Explain the distribution of soil and the crops grown in them
Understand the causes of soil erosion and prevention measures

key_terms.definition: Also called Regur Soil/Black Cotton Soil — formed from weathering of igneous rocks, best suited for cotton
Most extensively spread soil in India — formed by river deposits, fertile for rice, wheat, sugarcane
Widely distributed in peninsular plateau — deficient in nitrogen and salts
Found in areas with more than 200 cm of rainfall, red in colour, low fertility
Transportation of surface soil by various natural forces
Preventing soil erosion and maintaining its fertility

mcqs.question: The most extensively spread soil in India
mcqs.explanation: Alluvial soil is the most extensively spread soil in India, covering the Indo-Gangetic plains

mcqs.question: The soil which is also called Regur Soil is
mcqs.explanation: Black soil is also called Regur Soil and Black Cotton Soil

mcqs.question: The soil found in the Deccan Trap region is
mcqs.explanation: Black soil is found in the Deccan Trap region formed by volcanic activity

mcqs.question: Deficiency of nitrogen and salts is found in this soil
mcqs.explanation: Red soil is deficient in nitrogen and salts

one_mark_questions.question: Why is black soil called 'black cotton soil'?
one_mark_questions.answer: Black soil is best suited for cotton cultivation so it is also called Black Cotton Soil

one_mark_questions.question: What is meant by soil erosion?
one_mark_questions.answer: Transportation of surface soil by various natural forces is called soil erosion

one_mark_questions.question: The soil largely found in Rajasthan?

three_mark_questions.question: How can we prevent soil erosion?
